top_signals.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Traders Weigh Interest-Rate Outlook
top_signals.summary:
Gold steadied after the release of US economic data, as traders slightly pulled back on expectations for interest rate hikes.
Suki Cooper, Global Head: Commodities Research at Standard Chartered Bank, discusses her outlook on the precious metals space and why central banks are buying gold amid the pullback. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Iranian leader scoffs at Trump claim that Iran must buy U.S. farm products with unfrozen assets
top_signals.summary: "The only crop we're harvesting is what you planted: decades of mistrust," Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said in a social media post.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Supreme Court limits Roundup cancer suits against Bayer's Monsanto
top_signals.summary: Glyphosate, used in Roundup weedkiller, is the most commonly used weedkiller in agriculture, and it has long been linked to cancer claims.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
